entries.summary: Repo-local Coobie memory guide for Harkonnen-Labs
entries.content:
# Project Memory Guide

This directory is the durable home for knowledge that should travel with this repo.

Store here:
- domain facts specific to this product
- runtime/API details
- dataset and oracle semantics
- line-specific tuning or commissioning lessons
- accepted mitigations and known failure modes

Do not keep everything in Harkonnen core memory. Promote only durable cross-project patterns to the factory memory store.

entries.summary: Repeatable failure pattern in hidden_scenarios / sable via claude with anthropic/webapp-testing
entries.content:
Occurrences: 2
Supporting runs: d63dd0b2-1179-4b03-b1a4-be371fce5b47
Provider route: claude
Prompt bundle fingerprint: e8b6470a2c051708
Prompt bundle artifact: agents/sable_prompt_bundle.json
Pinned skills: anthropic/webapp-testing
Required checks: Visible validation must prove the main project still builds or executes in the staged workspace. | The twin narrative must state which external systems are simulated, stubbed, or still missing. | Revalidate stale memory lesson-exploration-hidden-scenarios-281ce3b5-9e46-43cd-a9aa-8f1c3de193f8 before relying on it; severity=high score=70. | Revalidate stale memory lesson-hidden-scenarios-281ce3b5-9e46-43cd-a9aa-8f1c3de193f8 before relying on it; severity=high score=70.
Guardrails: Require every planning agent to consume Coobie's briefing before finalizing its output. | Prefer explicit state machines and quality-coded data flows over implicit happy-path assumptions. | Capture evidence artifacts for each critical validation claim instead of relying on narrative confidence. | Retrieve and cite project memory topics before planning: capacity, fallback, provider, llm. | Account for recurring cause 'Broad implementation scope (many agent phases active, wide file changes) increases the probability of hidden scenario failures.' early instead of waiting for validation to rediscover it. | Apply distilled lesson before acting: Repeated failure pattern in hidden_scenarios: hidden_scenarios:sable:thasnotgrate thasrealnotgrate hidden scenario evaluation finished scenario s. | Apply distilled lesson before acting: Repeated failure pattern in hidden_scenarios: hidden_scenarios:sable:hidden scenario evaluation finished scenario s. | Treat high-risk stale project memories as provisional until Coobie’s mitigation steps are satisfied. | Do not rely on memory lesson-exploration-hidden-scenarios-281ce3b5-9e46-43cd-a9aa-8f1c3de193f8 as settled truth until the newer contradicting evidence is reviewed. | Do not rely on memory lesson-hidden-scenarios-281ce3b5-9e46-43cd-a9aa-8f1c3de193f8 as settled truth until the newer contradicting evidence is reviewed.
Memory ids: run-d63dd0b2-1179-4b03-b1a4-be371fce5b47 | 01-agent-roster | 02-setup-guide | 03-mcp-tools | 04-spec-format | coobie-openagent-roadmap | coobie-residue-roadmap | coobie-solitaire-agents-roadmap | imports__manual-drop-txt | lamdet-middle-peak-sling-note | reference-oracle-scenario-pattern
Relevant lesson ids: lesson-hidden_scenarios-281ce3b5-9e46-43cd-a9aa-8f1c3de193f8 | lesson-hidden_scenarios-79eaefb5-545a-4724-90f5-efd30b27496f | lesson-hidden_scenarios-af67a36a-ffbc-4774-a6ae-aabd475b01ec | lesson-hidden_scenarios-a4f5609e-3750-4c9c-ac15-cbfc8d5f689c | lesson-hidden_scenarios-f613d94c-bc6a-4bd9-ac5d-cdb7dd80452d
Query terms: wire-fallback-for | Wire CapacityState::fallback_for into build_provider_with_capacity | The fallback_for method on CapacityState is defined but never called. build_provider_with_capacity manually walks the fallback_chain instead of using it. This spec asks Mason to refactor build_provider_with_capacity so it uses fallback_for to resolve the next available provider, eliminating the dead code warning and keeping the two implementations in sync. | Harkonnen-Labs | path | refactor build_provider_with_capacity in src/llm.rs to call fallback_for | remove the manual fallback_chain walk that duplicates fallback_for logic | keep all existing provider-selection behavior intact | eliminate the dead_code warning on CapacityState::fallback_for | only modify src/llm.rs and src/capacity.rs | do not change the public API of build_provider or build_provider_with_capacity | do not change provider selection order or fallback behavior | all existing callers must continue to compile | src/llm.rs — build_provider_with_capacity implementation | src/capacity.rs — CapacityState and fallback_for definition | src/llm.rs with refactored build_provider_with_capacity | no compiler warnings about unused fallback_for | cargo check passes with no errors | fallback_for is called from build_provider_with_capacity | the dead_code warning on fallback_for is gone | provider selection behavior is unchanged | rust | cargo | cargo check should complete in under 60 seconds | no secrets or credentials introduced | llm_provider_builder | code_under_test | source_file | src/llm.rs | build_provider_with_capacity is the target function | currently walks fallback_chain manually instead of using fallback_for | capacity_state | src/capacity.rs | fallback_for is defined but never called (dead_code warning) | should be the single source of truth for fallback resolution | refactor_dead_code | wire fallback_for into build_provider_with_capacity and verify cargo check passes | capacity | fallback | provider | llm

This directory is the durable home for knowledge that should travel with this repo.

Store here:
- domain facts specific to this product
- runtime/API details
